Freshly ground black pepper plus more

instructions: A deep-fry thermometer
Corn flour, which is more finely ground than cornmeal, is available at Latin markets, natural foods stores, and bobsredmill.com.

Whisk egg yolk and 1 Tbsp. vinegar in a small bowl. Whisking constantly, slowly drizzle in oil, drop by drop at first, until mayonnaise is thickened and smooth. Add remaining 1 Tbsp. vinegar; season with salt and pepper. Cover and chill.
Mayonnaise can be made 1 day ahead. Keep chilled.
Fit a large pot with thermometer; pour in oil to measure 3”. Heat over medium-high heat until thermometer registers 375°.

Meanwhile, whisk all-purpose flour, baking powder, baking soda, 1 tsp. kosher salt, and ½ tsp. pepper in a large bowl. Whisking constantly, slowly add beer, club soda, and vinegar, adding more beer if batter is too thick (it should be the consistency of thin pancake batter).

Place corn flour in a shallow bowl. Season fish with kosher salt and pepper. Dredge fish in corn flour, shaking off excess, then dip in batter, letting excess drip back into bowl. Working in batches and returning oil to 375° between batches, fry fish until golden brown and crisp, about 2 minutes per side. Transfer to paper towel–lined baking sheet.
Season fish and fries with Old Bay, sea salt, and dill; serve with malt vinegar mayonnaise and lemon wedges.

instructions: Para comenzar, lavaremos bien los chiles. Luego les quitaremos las semillas y los restos de ramas.
To cook them, we'll warm up a pot with water. Once the liquid's boiling, we'll add the chilies and cook them for 10 minutes.

You lie so bad, we'll use our time to peel our garlic teeth. We will also peel a small onion and mince it into irregular pieces, to quickly pat it into a frying pan with an oil chorrit. We can do the same with our garlic teeth.
After 10 minutes of cooking the chilies, we will pass them to a blender with a little water with which we have boiled them, vinegar, garlic teeth, onion and a touch of salt, cumin and pepper.

We'll get a frying pan and we'll get the sauce. We'll cook about 5 to 10 minutes, what's necessary to evaporate some fluid and to have a more pitiful consistency. We'll put them back later.
We'll wash the fish well under a jet of water. It should be clear from scales, inks and open with a butterfly.
Seca el pescado y espolvorea un poco de sal. Cubre del lado de la carne con la salsa de chiles guajillos que acabamos de preparar.

Put the fish onto a coal grill (with the latter already on). You can help with a beaker or with an aluminum paper as a support and cover for fish. It's important to leave his face and his scales to his fire or his coils. The part with meat and salsa should be up. Make sure the fish isn't too close to the pits so that it doesn't get burned.
Cooking this way for 20 minutes, under constant supervision.

Cooking for another 20 minutes with constant supervision. Remember that many factors can interfere with frying: the size of the fish, its vicinity with the coils, the completely irregular temperatures of the grills, the quality of coal and a long and so forth.

Once frying has been completed, the fish at the height will be ready to serve. Can be accompanied with slices or a white or purple onion julian, grilled potatoes, a vegetable mix or a lettuce and tomato salad. Of course, don't forget to leave some pieces or cuts of lemon at hand.

flayed-man-cheese-ball.name: Flayed Man Cheese Ball
instructions: Lightly coat plastic face mask with cooking spray. Line the face mask with plastic wrap, taking care to press it down fully into the nose and eyes.

Reserve two or three slices of prosciutto and set aside. Tear remaining slices of prosciutto into strips and lay them into the mask to form the 'muscle tissue.' Start around the eyes, leaving eyes blank, and then create the cheeks, chin, and forehead.
Take the two whole green olives and position them in the eye holes, pimento side-down. Set the mask aside.

Combine the softened cream cheese, Cheddar, and Havarti cheeses in a mixing bowl. Add the chopped green olives and roasted red peppers. Use an electric mixer or clean hands to mix until thoroughly combined.

Take a heaping spoonful of cheese mixture and arrange it to form an eye around one olive, and then the other. Gently lay handfuls of the cheese mixture all around the mask, taking care not to move the slices of prosciutto. Press cheese down as you work to fill in all of the crevices. When mask is full, gently press in cheese mixture all around the mask. Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.

Invert the mask onto a serving platter and gently remove the mask and plastic wrap. You should be left with a gorgeous (and freaky) face. If there is cheese showing around the bottom edges, use the reserved prosciutto slices to cover it before serving.

freekeh-salad.name: Freekeh Salad

ingredients.item: Pomegranate molasses can be found at middle eastern markets

instructions: Cook freekeh in a large saucepan of simmering salted water until tender, 30–40 minutes. Drain and rinse under cold water to stop cooking.
Whisk lemon juice, oil, and pomegranate molasses in a large bowl. Add freekeh, onion, parsley, and two-thirds of cherry tomatoes; season with salt and toss to combine.
Transfer salad to a platter and top with remaining tomatoes and savory leaves and/or mustard flowers, if using.
Do Ahead: Salad (without savory and mustard flowers) can be made 6 hours ahead. Cover and chill.

french-macarons-with-burnt-caramel-filli.name: French Macarons With Burnt Caramel Filling

ingredients.prep: or 3/4 cup sifted almond flour and 1/4 cup sifted hazelnut flour

instructions: Line 2 large baking sheets with parchment. Sift powdered sugar, almond flour, and hazelnut flour (if using) into large bowl. Using electric mixer, beat egg whites, sugar, and pinch of salt in medium bowl until medium peaks form. Add egg white mixture to almond mixture; fold to incorporate. Working in 2 batches, fill pastry bag fitted with 1/4-inch-diameter plain pastry tip with batter (batter will be thin and will drip from bag). Pipe batter in 11/4-inch rounds on baking sheets, spacing 1 inch apart (cookies will spread slightly). Let rest on sheets at room temperature 20 minutes.

Position 1 rack in top third and 1 rack in bottom third of oven; preheat to 191°C. Bake cookies 5 minutes. Reduce oven temperature to 163°C. Continue to bake cookies until puffed and golden on top, about 10 minutes, reversing sheets after 5 minutes. Cool cookies on sheets on rack. Carefully peel cookies from parchment. DO AHEAD Can be made 1 day ahead. Store in airtight container at room temperature.

Stir sugar, honey, 3 tablespoons water, and 1/4 teaspoon salt in large saucepan over medium-low heat until sugar dissolves. Increase heat to high and boil without stirring until syrup is dark amber, occasionally brushing down sides of pan with wet pastry brush and swirling pan, about 8 minutes. Remove from heat and immediately add cream and butter (mixture will bubble vigorously). Return pan to stovetop. Attach candy thermometer to side of pan. Boil caramel over medium-high heat until candy thermometer registers 112°C, 8 to 10 minutes. Transfer caramel filling to medium bowl; cool to room temperature.

Line rimmed baking sheet with parchment paper. Spoon 1 teaspoon caramel filling onto bottom (flat side) of 1 macaroon. Top with second macaroon, flat-side down, pressing slightly to adhere and gently pressing filling almost to edge of macaroon. Place on prepared sheet. Repeat with remaining filling and macaroons. Cover and refrigerate overnight. DO AHEAD Can be made 2 days ahead. Store in airtight container in refrigerator. Let macaroons stand at room temperature 30 minutes before serving.

fried-eggplant-with-crispy-basil-and-whi.name: Fried Eggplant With Crispy Basil And Whipped Honey Feta

ingredients.item: Diamond crystal or  0.25 tsp. morton kosher salt

instructions: Cut 1 large globe eggplant (about 1 lb.) lengthwise into ½"-thick slabs. Cut each slab crosswise into 3–4" pieces, then cut each piece lengthwise into ½"-thick strips to make fries.

Place 1 cup (125 g) all-purpose flour in a shallow bowl. Place 3 large eggs in another shallow bowl and beat lightly to blend. Place 3 cups panko in a third shallow bowl. Using a measuring cup, crush panko into slightly finer pieces (you don’t need an even consistency, just some finer and coarser bits). Season each component with kosher salt.

Working with a few at a time, dredge eggplant fries in flour, shaking off excess. Dip in egg, letting excess drip back into bowl, then coat in panko, pressing gently to adhere. Transfer to a baking sheet.

Pour vegetable oil into a large Dutch oven or other heavy pot fitted with thermometer to come 2" up sides. Heat over medium-high until thermometer registers 350°. Working in batches and bringing oil back up to 350° between batches, fry eggplant, turning occasionally, until golden brown and crisp on all sides, about 3 minutes. Transfer to a wire rack set inside another rimmed baking sheet; season immediately with salt.

Add 5 basil leaves to oil (be careful, oil may spatter). Using a long-handled wooden spoon, push basil down into oil to submerge (this will help stop spattering). Fry until leaves are dark green and crisp, about 1 minute. Transfer to rack with eggplant; season with salt. Repeat with remaining 5 basil leaves and more salt.

Process 7 oz. feta, room temperature, in a food processor until as smooth as possible (it will still be quite crumbly). Add ⅓ cup heavy cream, 2 tsp. honey, remaining ½ tsp. Diamond Crystal or ¼ tsp. Morton kosher salt, and lots of freshly ground black pepper. Process until smooth. Transfer to a small bowl.

Transfer eggplant fries to plates and top with fried basil. Serve with whipped feta alongside for dipping.


Editor’s note: Head this way for more of our best eggplant recipes →

fried-potato-skin-with-shrimp-dip-telecu.name: Fried Potato Skin With Shrimp Dip
fried-potato-skin-with-shrimp-dip-telecu.original_name: Casca de batata frita com dip de camarão
instructions: Cook the shrimp kernel in water, drain it and let it cool. Then put it in a processor and add the mascarpone cheese and Greek yogurt.
Add the mustard, lemon juice and parsley (reserving some of this for decoration) and season with salt, pink pepper and paprika. Crush everything until you get a homogeneous preparation. Reserve.
After well washed and run, fry potato peels in abundant hot oil. Spill on absorbent paper and season with salt.
Serve the potato peels with the shrimp dip, placed in a separate saucer, splashing everything with the remaining chopped parsley.

fried-zucchini.name: Fried Zucchini
instructions: Set up three separate bowls. One with flour, one with egg mixed with milk, and one with bread crumbs.
Cut zucchini in sticks or rounds.
Dip in flour, then egg mix, then bread crumbs.
Fry in oil over medium-high heat, turning a few times, until golden.
Sprinkle with salt and parmesan. Serve immediately with ranch dip.

frozen-mudslide.name: Frozen Mudslide
instructions: Combine crushed ice, vodka, coffee liqueur, and Irish cream liqueur in a blender. Drizzle in chocolate syrup. Blend until smooth.
Pour into glasses and garnish with whipped cream.

fruit-dip.name: Fruit Dip
instructions: Gather cream cheese and marshmallow creme.
Place cream cheese and creme in a medium bowl.
Blend cream cheese and marshmallow creme with an electric mixer until smooth and well combined.
Serve with fruit and enjoy!

fudge-nut-oatmeal-bars.name: Fudge Nut Oatmeal Bars
instructions: Preheat oven to 177°C (175 degrees C).

Beat 1 cup butter and brown sugar in a large bowl until smooth and creamy. Stir in eggs, 2 teaspoons vanilla extract, and 1/2 teaspoon almond extract; mix well. Combine oats, flour, sliced almonds, baking soda, and 1 teaspoon salt in another bowl; stir into butter mixture until batter is just mixed. Spread 2/3 batter into a 10x15-inch rimmed baking sheet.

Heat chocolate chips, condensed milk, 2 tablespoons butter, and 1/2 teaspoon salt in a saucepan over medium heat until chocolate is melted, about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Remove from heat and stir in remaining 2 teaspoons vanilla extract and 1/2 teaspoon almond extract. Spread chocolate mixture into baking sheet over oat batter. Spoon remaining 1/3 oat batter over chocolate.
Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, 20 to 25 minutes.

funnel-cake-waffles.name: Funnel Cake Waffles
instructions: Combine 1/2 cup warm water and 1 teaspoon sugar in a very large mixing bowl; add yeast. Let stand until yeast softens and begins to form a creamy foam, about 10 minutes.
Add flour, 2 cups water, melted butter, and salt to yeast mixture; stir until smooth. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate, 8 hours to overnight; dough will rise quite a bit.
When ready to make waffles, stir 1/4 cup sugar, vanilla, and baking soda into batter until smooth.
Preheat a waffle iron according to manufacturer's instructions. Lightly grease the hot iron.
Working in batches, pour in about 1/4 cup batter for each waffle. Cook until waffles are golden brown and the iron stops steaming, 5 to 10 minutes.
